# EMURGO / SecondFi Public Statements — Citation Record

`public_reference` evidence. Secondary-source citation record of EMURGO's /
SecondFi's public statements about the incident, captured 2026-07-02. These are
**news-outlet reports** summarizing EMURGO/SecondFi statements, not a capture of
EMURGO's own primary channel. The primary statements (EMURGO/SecondFi blog,
support site, official social accounts) should be archived directly to harden
these into primary evidence — see `EMURGO_ACCOUNTABILITY.md` §8.

All quotes verbatim from the linked articles as fetched 2026-07-02.

## Commitment to refund affected users, on a two-week timeline

- The Block, "SecondFi maps recovery path after $2.4 million Cardano wallet
  exploit, aims to return funds within two weeks", 2026-06-27
  <https://www.theblock.co/post/406457/secondfi-maps-recovery-path-after-2-4-million-cardano-wallet-exploit-aims-to-return-funds-within-two-weeks>
  - "identified what he called 'a clear recovery solution.' He put the timeline
    at roughly two weeks"
  - "one week to build the recovery mechanism and a second to test it before any
    returns begin"
- The Crypto Times, "Cardano's SecondFi Hack: EMURGO Sets 2-Week Timeline to
  Return Stolen ADA", 2026-06-27
  <https://www.cryptotimes.io/2026/06/27/cardanos-secondfi-hack-emurgo-sets-2-week-timeline-to-return-stolen-ada/>
  - "two-week timeline before assets can begin being returned to affected users"
  - "first week will be dedicated to building the recovery tool itself, while the
    second week will focus on thorough testing and security reviews"

## Claim that ~129M ADA was moved to an independent third-party custodian

- The Block (2026-06-27):
  "moved about 129 million ADA to an independent third-party custodian as an
  emergency measure"
- The Crypto Times (2026-06-27):
  "the fourth was an emergency intervention by the SecondFi team itself, which
  secured approximately 129 million ADA by moving the funds to a third-party
  custodian as a precautionary measure"
- Cryptopolitan, "SecondFi takes final snapshot of users funds, recommits to
  refund priority"
  <https://www.cryptopolitan.com/secondfi-takes-final-snapshot-of-users-funds-recommits-to-refund-priority/>
  — final balance snapshot taken 2026-06-26; reimbursement fund launched; "an
  external accounting firm has been engaged to verify those holdings"; affected
  users can file claims through the support site.

## No custodian or accounting firm named

- Both The Block and The Crypto Times refer only generically to "an independent
  third-party custodian" and "an external accounting firm has been engaged to
  verify." **Neither the custodian nor the accounting firm is named** in any
  reviewed source as of capture.

## Cause acknowledged; scope statements

- The Crypto Times (2026-06-27):
  "The exploit was traced to a vulnerability in SecondFi's native Cardano web
  wallet generation software, the component responsible for creating wallets and
  managing private keys." Hoskinson is quoted classifying it as "an
  application-level security failure confined to SecondFi."
- Public loss figure attributed to the three external-attacker events:
  ~16M ADA / 374 addresses (multiple outlets). This repository's own confirmed-
  theft cluster measures 12,193,786.56 ADA / 179 source stakes; the
  reconciliation is open (see `EMURGO_ACCOUNTABILITY.md` §4).

## Capture method

Fetched via URL-to-markdown extraction on 2026-07-02; quotes copied verbatim from
the fetched content. Because these are client-servable news pages that can change,
treat this as a point-in-time secondary record. Recommended hardening: archive the
primary EMURGO/SecondFi statements and file a web-archive snapshot of each URL.
